channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Universal secures rights for film adaptations of 10 Atari games
item.description: Gear up for the Atari Cinematic Universe.

item.title: Adata boss says DRAM shortage to last another decade with no AI bubble bursting before 2030
item.description: Adata Chairman Chen Li-bai expects AI demand to sustain global memory shortages despite factory expansions and sharply rising DDR5 prices worldwide.

item.title: Le télescope James-Webb a vu des centaines de « points rouges » : ce qu’ils pourraient réellement cacher est vertigineux
item.description: Il existe plusieurs objets et phénomènes exotiques en astrophysique qui sont des serpents de mer, comme on dit. Des signes de leur existence apparaissent parfois et à répétition dans les observations, sans que l'on puisse aboutir vraiment à une conclusion. C'est le cas avec une nouvelle...

item.title: Instagram is now banning users who make creepy content with Meta glasses
item.description: Meta is finally cracking down on people users who harass and record people with its smart glasses.

item.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
item.description: Claude's new voice model will let you reschedule your meeting or draft an email

item.title: Claude's voice mode just got smarter
item.description: Claude's voice mode hasn't been the most reliable, but this update should help.

item.title: Meta’s New Feel-Good AI Ad Uses a Song About the World Ending
item.description: The clip features the David Bowie track “Five Years,” which includes lyrics such as “Earth was really dying (dying).”

item.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
item.description: The Series A was led by Battery Ventures, bringing AegisAI total funding to $49 million.

item.title: « Je ne recommande à personne d’en acheter » : la grogne et le désespoir des utilisateurs de vélos électriques Cowboy six mois après le rachat par Rebirth
item.description: Assistance qui se coupe toutes les cinq minutes, connecteur en rupture de stock pendant six mois, service client qui ne répond plus : trois utilisateurs parisiens de vélos électriques Cowboy racontent leur quotidien depuis la reprise de la marque belge par le groupe français Rebirth, et leur expérience pré-rachat. Nous avons soumis ces signalements à Grégory Trebaol, le patron du groupe, qui en conteste une partie. ...

item.title: Here's why Chinese smartwatches like the Oppo Watch X3 are rarely sold in the US
item.description: The device is ready; the business isn't.

item.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
item.description: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of its workers, or around 93 employees, as reported earlier by 404 Media. In a memo to employees, Patreon CEO Jack Conte writes that the company isn't making these changes "because we believe AI replaces humans," but says AI has "fundamentally transformed the tech industry, including how we work, […]

item.title: For Taylor Swift, Madison Square Garden’s Controversial Cameras Briefly Went Dark
item.description: MSG’s sprawling surveillance system can monitor guests down to the second. Its owners made an exception for the pop star’s rehearsal dinner.

item.title: Menacée de fermeture, cette usine Ford en Europe sauvée in extremis grâce à une alliance avec la Chine et Geely
item.description: Ford officialise une alliance avec le chinois Geely pour maintenir en vie son usine d'Almussafes, en Espagne. Une coentreprise doit démarrer sa production en 2028, mais au-delà de l'annonce, le constructeur américain reste avare de détails sur ce que cela signifie réellement pour l'avenir du site et de ses emplois.

item.title: Experts have found a trojan able to rig online live betting platforms
item.description: A company building betting software was targeted with a rather sneaky trojan.

item.title: How do we remain human while using AI?
item.description: Muneer Panjwani says that AI can synthesize information at remarkable speed, but creativity, intuition, and original thinking remain distinctly human. Muneer Panjwani says that AI can synthesize information at remarkable speed, but creativity, intuition, and original thinking remain distinctly human.

item.title: Bethesda accused of breaking Canadian labor laws with Montreal layoffs
item.description: The fallout from the big Xbox layoffs continues to develop.

item.title: I was impressed by the spec of this budget gaming keyboard — but there’s one feature it desperately needs
item.description: The Keychron K2 Ultra 8K has the spec of a top-tier mechanical keyboard without the high price tag — but can it really challenge the best of them?

item.title: I asked a disaster survival psychologist if AI usage should be the same level of 'emergency' as Pompeii — but instead she's worried about a technology catastrophe that I didn't even consider
item.description: It's not every day you get to talk to a survival psychologist in a hit National Geographic documentary about your technology fears — but how worried should we be by the rise of AI?

item.title: The hidden penalty working mothers pay
item.description: Parental leave looks great on paper, but many working mothers who take it have seen missed promotions, unfair performance reviews, or even job loss. Nearly half of all Equal Employment Opportunity Commission lawsuits last year involved pregnancy-related claims. Here’s what’s happening to women in the workplace and how companies need to change. Parental leave looks great on paper, but many working mothers who take it have seen missed promotions, unfair performance reviews, or even job loss. Nearly half of all Equal Employment Opportunity Commission lawsuits last year involved pregnancy-related...

item.title: U.S. filings for unemployment aid fall to lowest level since 1969
item.description: The number of Americans applying for unemployment benefits in the week ending July 18 declined by 22,000 to 187,000. U.S. applications for jobless benefits tumbled to the lowest level in more than five decades last week as layoffs remain historically low despite global economic uncertainty.

item.title: Black Forest Labs launches FLUX 3 capable of generating images and 20-second video with audio — but in limited release to start
item.description: Black Forest Labs (BFL) is expanding its FLUX family beyond image generation with today's launch of FLUX 3, a multimodal frontier model trained to understand and generate images, or combined audio/video clips up to 20 seconds from a single prompt — and to extend the same underlying architecture to robotic vision and actions.The Freiburg, Germany-based AI lab says FLUX 3 is jointly trained across those modalities rather than assembling separate image, video and audio models behind a common interface. That distinction is central to the company's pitch: BFL wants enterprises to think about creati...

item.title: Tinder expands its live events feature to more cities as dating app burnout grips Gen Z
item.description: The Match Group brand has discovered which types of events are resonating most. One 60-person candlelight ceramics class sold out within a day. Tinder has announced a global expansion of Tinder Events, its in-app feature designed to help singles connect IRL through shared interests.

item.title: The skill every young creative needs
item.description: Bo Zhao believes curiosity is built through conversations, not algorithms. The best creative education happens in the real world. Bo Zhao believes curiosity is built through conversations, not algorithms. The best creative education happens in the real world.

item.title: EU gives antritrust approval to the Saudi-led $55 billion takeover of EA
item.description: The deal isn't done yet, but it's getting closer.

item.title: AI doesn’t solve creativity
item.description: AI can explore countless directions, but it can’t replace the intuition required to choose the right one. AI can explore countless directions, but it can’t replace the intuition required to choose the right one.

item.title: The CEO with a ‘Peaky Blinders’ playbook
item.description: Gordon Boggis argues that creativity isn’t something you can teach—it’s something you unlock through curiosity, experience, and the courage to think differently. Gordon Boggis argues that creativity isn’t something you can teach—it’s something you unlock through curiosity, experience, and the courage to think differently.

item.title: Amazfit Cheetah 2 Pro data reveals how Josh Kerr just shattered the 27-year-old mile world record —and his split stats explain the historic run
item.description: Amazfit has published data from Josh Kerr’s world-record mile time and revealed the secrets to his success.

item.title: « Nous resterons à la pointe » : la pique subtile de Samsung contre Apple juste avant l’iPhone Ultra
item.description: Après avoir présenté son Galaxy Z Fold 8, Samsung tacle subtilement Apple, sans le nommer, en affirmant que personne ne peut égaler son expertise dans le secteur des smartphones pliables du jour au lendemain.

item.title: Great leaders know when to say no
item.description: Deirdre White believes progress comes from making tough choices and focusing creative energy where it can have the greatest impact. Deirdre White believes progress comes from making tough choices and focusing creative energy where it can have the greatest impact.

item.title: Google will now let you log in with a selfie
item.description: Good news for the chronically forgetful; bad news for people attempting fraud. To keep accounts safe from malicious intruders, having various passwords and authentication steps has become the norm. With that comes an oh-so-common predicament: doing such a good job securing an account that even you can’t get back in.

item.title: Bah, humbug: Johnny Depp wipes his grid for Ebenezer
item.description: Johnny Depp wiped his Instagram and replaced it with three posts teasing "Ebenezer: A Christmas Carol," in theaters November 13.

item.title: US government says Iran-linked hackers are disrupting American water and energy providers
item.description: An updated government advisory warns that Iranian hackers are exploiting systems used by water and energy providers.
